`,(0,C.jsx)(i,{caption:"Figure 2: Basic sketch of the stock trading mobile app",children:(0,C.jsx)(n,{src:V,alt:"Image represents a three-screen mobile application workflow for stock trading. The first screen displays the user's portfolio, showing 'Funds:', a portfolio performance graph indicating upward ('Portfolio \u2191') and downward ('\u2193') trends for 'Stock 1' and 'Stock 2' respectively, and a 'Last orders' section listing executed and open trades with stock symbols and prices.  A curved arrow connects this screen to the second screen, indicating data transfer. The second screen focuses on a specific stock ('Stock 1'), displaying its current price ('$ x.yz'), a price graph, and empty fields (likely for order details).  'BUY' and 'SELL' buttons are present. A filled circle on the 'SELL' button indicates the current interaction point.  Another curved arrow connects the 'SELL' button to the third screen. The third screen shows the same stock ('Stock 1') and price ('$ x.yz'), its graph, and a shaded section for order confirmation. This section includes 'Market price: $x.yz', fields for 'Quantity' and 'Total value' (both empty for user input), and a 'SELL' button to finalize the transaction.  The data flow is unidirectional: portfolio information feeds into the stock detail screen, and the order details from the stock detail screen are passed to the order confirmation screen.",width:"602",height:"308"})}),`

`,(0,C.jsx)(i,{caption:"Figure 3: Stock trading high-level mobile architecture",children:(0,C.jsx)(n,{src:d,alt:"Image represents a mobile application architecture diagram.  The diagram is divided into a client-side and a server-side. The client-side, labeled 'Client,' contains a 'UI layer' with components: 'Home Screen,' 'Stock Detail Screen,' 'Place Order Modal,' and 'Image Loader.'  Below these are corresponding 'State Holders' for each screen, managing their respective application states.  These UI components interact with a 'Data layer' containing 'Portfolio Repository,' 'Stocks Repository,' and 'Orders Repository,' which in turn access 'Portfolio Remote Data Source,' 'Stocks Remote Data Source,' and 'Orders Remote Data Source' respectively.  The data flow is facilitated by 'Dependency Injection.'  The server-side includes an 'API Gateway' communicating with a 'Backend' and a 'CDN' (Content Delivery Network) via HTTPS. The 'API Gateway' uses HTTPS for communication with the client and WSS (WebSockets) for real-time updates with the 'Real Time Updates Dispatcher.' The 'Backend' also interacts with a 'Third Party Push Notifications Provider' via HTTPS to send push notifications, managed by the client-side 'Push Notifications Client.'  The 'HTTP Network Dispatcher' handles HTTP requests.  The 'Navigator' component in the UI layer manages navigation between screens.  Data flows from the remote data sources to the repositories, then to the state holders, and finally to the UI components.  The image loader fetches images from the CDN.",width:"602",height:"471"})}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"Let's see how the key components of our architecture interact with each other."}),`

`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`

`,(0,C.jsx)(e.h4,{id:"api-gateway",children:"API Gateway"}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"In our stock trading app, security is non-negotiable. We need robust authentication, careful request monitoring, and strict policy enforcement\u2014all while maintaining a seamless user experience. This is where the API Gateway [2] plays a crucial role."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"Serving as our system's front door, the API Gateway validates and routes all incoming client requests to the appropriate backend services. It blocks any suspicious or unauthorized requests before they can reach our core systems."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"The API Gateway handles several key responsibilities:"}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Security: It manages user authentication and authorization, implements rate limiting, and detects suspicious clients to protect our system."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Traffic management: It balances load across services and controls request volumes to prevent system overload during peak trading hours."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Request processing: It validates all incoming requests, transforms data formats when needed, and manages request/response headers to ensure proper communication."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Monitoring: It tracks API usage, performance metrics, and error rates to maintain system health and identify potential issues early."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Simplification: It provides a clean, unified API that shields mobile clients from the complexity of our backend architecture."}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.h3,{id:"client-architecture",children:"Client architecture"}),`

`,(0,C.jsxs)("div",{className:"note-block",children:[(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:(0,C.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"\u{1F6E0}\uFE0F Platform implementation details"})}),(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"Buffered updates require efficient data stream management that needs to be sent through the JavaScript bridge to update the WebView."}),(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"On Android, we can leverage:"}),(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"The JavascriptInterface mechanism to establish reliable communication between native code and WebView charts."}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"Kotlin Flows with appropriate dispatchers to handle buffering and throttling off the main thread."}),`
`]}),`
`]}),(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"On iOS, we can leverage:"}),(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"WKUserContentController and WKScriptMessageHandler enable native-to-JavaScript communication."}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"Combine framework's throttle and debounce operators to control update frequency."}),`
`]}),`
`]})]}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.h4,{id:"real-time-chart-update-data-flow",children:"Real-time chart update data flow"}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"Figure 6 maps the data journey when a user opens a stock's intraday chart, updating at real time:"}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ol,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"The Stocks remote data source fetches the current day's historical data via a REST API call to establish the baseline chart data."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Simultaneously, the remote data source establishes a WebSocket connection for receiving real-time price updates."}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:["The Stocks repository combines these two sources and implements the buffered updates strategy:",`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ol,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"The repository creates the data stream and emits the current day's historical data as the initial value."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"As WebSocket updates arrive, they're collected in a buffer within the repository."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"The repository doesn't send each update immediately to subscribers. Instead, it batches these updates and emits them at regular intervals."}),`
`]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"The Stock detail state holder subscribes to this buffered data stream and is responsible for communicating with the JavaScript bridge to update the chart as new data arrives."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"The WebView chart renders these updates, creating the appearance of smooth, real-time data visualization."}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(i,{caption:"Figure 6: Intraday stock value chart data flow",children:(0,C.jsx)(n,{src:p,alt:"Image represents a system architecture diagram for displaying intraday stock values.  A user initiates the process by requesting to 'See intraday stock value graph,' which triggers a request to the 'Stock Detail Screen.' This screen interacts with a 'Stock Detail State Holder,' which in turn communicates with a 'Stocks Repository.' The 'Stocks Repository' receives data from two sources: 'HTTP Network Dispatcher' (1) and 'Real Time Updates Dispatcher' (2), both feeding into a 'Stocks Remote DataSource.'  The 'Stock Detail State Holder' (3) then sends data to a 'JavaScript Bridge' (4), which finally updates the 'Chart Rendering WebView' (5) to display the intraday stock graph to the user.  The numbered annotations (1-5) indicate the flow of data between components.",width:"562",height:"444"})}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.h4,{id:"common-bottlenecks-and-optimizations",children:"Common bottlenecks and optimizations"}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"Several common issues can still affect real-time UI performance:"}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[(0,C.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"WebView-related bottlenecks"}),". Frequent updates to the WebView can trigger expensive rendering operations. To minimize this impact, we:",`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Configure the charting library to use partial redraws, which update only the changed elements."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Disable animations during high-frequency updates to reduce rendering overhead."}),`
`]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[(0,C.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Data processing bottlenecks"}),". Processing incoming market data can sometimes become a bottleneck, especially when it involves complex calculations. To minimize this impact, we:",`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Move all data transformations to native code and execute them in a background thread before passing the data to the WebView."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Pre-compute derived values rather than calculating them in JavaScript."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Implement caching for computed values to prevent redundant calculations."}),`
`]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[(0,C.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Bridge communication overhead"}),". Every call between native code and WebView has a cost. We optimize this by:",`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Batching multiple price updates into a single bridge communication."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Using compact data formats to reduce serialization/deserialization costs."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Maintaining a consistent update interval that balances freshness with performance."}),`
`]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[(0,C.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Intermittent connectivity"}),". Mobile networks are inherently unreliable. When connectivity issues occur:",`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Clearly indicate when the data is stale due to connection loss."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Implement exponential backoff for reconnection attempts."}),`
`]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[(0,C.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Battery impact"}),". Real-time updates can significantly impact device battery life. We should:",`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Stop real-time updates when the app is in the background."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Implement adaptive throttling based on battery level."}),`
`]}),`
`]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"As our app grows in complexity and user base, our real-time update system needs to scale accordingly. Several aspects of our design ensure this scalability:"}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[(0,C.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"WebSocket message filtering"}),": We implement server-side filtering to ensure clients only receive the specific data they need, reducing client-side processing as the app adds features."]}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[(0,C.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Buffer size"}),": Our buffer needs to be large enough to accommodate all price updates between refresh intervals, even during periods of high market volatility. For most stocks, a buffer capacity of 10-20 price points should be sufficient."]}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.li,{children:[(0,C.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Adaptive scheduling"}),": We can implement adaptive scheduling that adjusts update frequency based on market conditions, device state, and user engagement when the user is actively looking at their data and the app is in the foreground:",`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Increase frequency during market hours with high volatility."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Allow users to adjust the frequency based on battery level and device performance."}),`
`]}),`
`]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)("div",{className:"note-block",children:[(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:(0,C.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"\u{1F50D} Industry insights:"})}),(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"Institutions such as J.P. Morgan or Commerzbank, and trading platforms like Binomo, use SciChart's [9] native engine to power real-time stock dashboards [10]."}),(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"Coinbase's exchange platform exposes a WebSocket feed to broadcast live market data for orders and trades [11]."})]}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.h2,{id:"step-5-wrap-up",children:"Step 5: Wrap-up"}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"In this chapter, we've designed a mobile stock trading app that delivers core functionality for investment management. Our system supports viewing real-time stock prices, executing trades through placing and canceling orders, analyzing historical price data through charts, and receiving timely price alerts."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"The architecture combines REST APIs for standard operations with WebSockets for real-time updates. We've implemented a layered client architecture that separates UI and data concerns, making the codebase both maintainable and testable. We also covered creating both historical and real-time intraday stock value charts."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.p,{children:"For those preparing to discuss this in a Mobile System Design interview, consider exploring these extensions to deepen the conversation:"}),`
`,(0,C.jsxs)(e.ul,{children:[`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Offline functionality: Designing a system for caching and local operation when network connectivity is limited."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Cross-platform considerations: Discussing how the architecture would adapt to support web and other platforms"}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Regulatory Compliance: Address the specific compliance requirements and regulations that govern stock trading in different regions."}),`
`,(0,C.jsx)(e.li,{children:"Personalized UI: Offer customizable dashboards or watchlists, enhancing engagement through tailored experiences."}),`
`]}),`
